key_findings.finding: CV(r*) under FixedWD is very high (2.36-3.49) across all architectures, strongly refuting the prediction that r* is uniform (CV < 0.15) under FixedWD. Even with constant WD, per-layer r* varies enormously.
key_findings.implication: The prediction that FixedWD produces uniform r* is wrong — even at 2 epochs, different layers have very different gradient-to-weight ratios due to architecture effects.

key_findings.finding: For ResNet-50, original UDWDC shows strong anti-correlation (Spearman=-0.61, p<0.001) but UDWDC-v2 does NOT (Spearman=-0.16, p=0.25). The EMA smoothing + floor clipping in v2 may be reducing the differentiation effect.
key_findings.implication: The stability fix (v2) trades off fixed-point differentiation for training stability. This is an important design tension to document.

key_findings.finding: ResNet-101 with UDWDC-v2 shows the STRONGEST anti-correlation (Spearman=-0.79, p<0.001), contradicting the earlier pilot that reported failure for ResNet-101.
key_findings.implication: Earlier pilot may have used corrupted data or different UDWDC version. Full training with more epochs may clarify.

key_findings.finding: ViT-S/16 with UDWDC-v2 shows marginal anti-correlation (Spearman=-0.32, p=0.025), barely passing the threshold.
key_findings.implication: H5 holds weakly for LayerNorm architectures, consistent with the hypothesis that BN layers show stronger differentiation.

key_findings.finding: Pearson correlations are very high for ResNet models (|r|>0.94) even under FixedWD, but Spearman is moderate. This suggests outlier layers (fc.weight has extreme r*) dominate Pearson but not Spearman.
key_findings.implication: Rank-based Spearman is the correct metric; Pearson is misleading due to fc.weight outlier.

risks: Only 2 pilot epochs — fixed-point equilibria not yet reached. Full 200/90-epoch results may differ significantly.
UDWDC-v2 Spearman failure on ResNet-50 may be a pilot artifact or a genuine design flaw of the floor clipping.
High CV(r*) under FixedWD contradicts theoretical prediction — need to investigate whether this is a training-start transient.
notes: Pilot data uses 2 epochs only. H5 analysis is primarily about steady-state behavior (last 10 epochs of full training). Pilot results should be interpreted cautiously.
